summary: Chatbot returns ticket answers without citations
description: When users ask for a status summary, the assistant sometimes answers with ticket facts but omits the source ticket IDs. Compliance review requires every ticket claim to cite the ticket key.
acceptance_criteria: Every Jira-related answer includes at least one ticket key citation.
If retrieved context has no supporting ticket, the assistant says there is not enough information.
Regression tests cover general questions and specific ticket questions.

comments.author: Ava Patel
comments.body: Observed in five of twelve sampled answers from production logs.

comments.author: Maya Chen
comments.body: Prompt guard is added locally. Need retrieval tests before merge.

custom_fields.customer_impact: High - compliance review failed
custom_fields.root_cause_hypothesis: Prompt permits uncited synthesis when context contains multiple tickets.

summary: Implement Jira API pagination for ticket sync
description: The sync job previously fetched only the first page of Jira search results. Add pagination using the startAt and maxResults fields until all issues for the project are fetched.
acceptance_criteria: Sync fetches all pages until total results are exhausted.
Logs include total fetched ticket count.
Unit test mocks three pages of Jira responses.

comments.author: Jordan Lee
comments.body: Pagination merged and verified against staging project AICB.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.body: QA confirmed 362 tickets synced instead of 50.

custom_fields.customer_impact: Medium - historical tickets were missing from analysis

summary: Add embedding cache invalidation when tickets are updated
description: Embeddings are reused even after a Jira ticket description or comments change. Cache keys should include the ticket updated_at value and a content fingerprint.
acceptance_criteria: Unchanged tickets reuse existing embeddings.
Changed tickets regenerate embeddings on the next sync.
Cache invalidation is covered by unit tests.

comments.author: Maya Chen
comments.body: This is currently unassigned and blocks reliable stale-index analysis.

custom_fields.customer_impact: Medium - users can receive answers from outdated ticket text

summary: Vector index returns stale ticket status after Jira transition
description: After AICB-087 moved from In Progress to Done, search results continued to show the old status for two hours. The vector index was rebuilt manually to resolve the mismatch.
acceptance_criteria: Status transitions trigger index refresh for the changed ticket.
Search results never show an older status than Jira API data.
Indexer logs include changed ticket keys.

comments.author: Noah Singh
comments.body: Root cause points to missing status in the content hash.

comments.author: Elena Brooks
comments.body: Redis write access is still pending from platform team.

custom_fields.customer_impact: High - executives saw stale completion status
custom_fields.blocked_reason: Waiting for Redis write access in production

summary: Evaluate hybrid keyword and vector retrieval for Jira questions
description: Research whether BM25 keyword retrieval combined with vector similarity improves ticket recall for IDs, component names, labels, and broad semantic questions.
acceptance_criteria: Compare vector-only and hybrid retrieval on at least 40 labeled queries.
Document precision and recall tradeoffs.
Recommend default top_k and reranking settings.

comments.author: Sam Rivera
comments.body: Initial hybrid run improved exact component questions by 18 percent.

comments.author: Ravi Kumar
comments.body: Please include ticket ID direct lookup in the evaluation table.

custom_fields.customer_impact: Low - evaluation only

summary: Direct ticket lookup fails for lowercase ticket keys
description: A query such as 'what is wrong with aicb-104' does not trigger direct lookup. The key extractor only matches uppercase project keys.
acceptance_criteria: Ticket key extraction is case-insensitive.
Specific ticket queries bypass semantic search when the ticket exists.
Invalid ticket IDs return a friendly not-found message.

comments.author: Lina Gomez
comments.body: Regex fix is small, but tests need examples for lowercase and mixed case.

custom_fields.customer_impact: Medium - users receive broad answers instead of exact tickets

summary: Add grounded root cause analysis response template
description: Root cause responses should separate ticket facts, inferred causes, missing evidence, and suggested validation steps. The model must not present guesses as facts.
acceptance_criteria: Template has separate sections for facts, inference, missing information, and confidence.
All factual claims cite ticket keys.
Snapshot tests verify wording for incomplete tickets.

comments.author: Owen Wright
comments.body: Draft template is ready; waiting for review from compliance.

custom_fields.customer_impact: Low - improves answer quality

summary: Repeated LLM provider 429 errors during daily ticket report
description: The 9 AM daily report retries immediately after rate limit responses. This caused three failed report runs in the last week.
acceptance_criteria: LLM retries use exponential backoff with jitter.
Daily report degrades gracefully when rate limits persist.
Failure count metric is emitted for alerting.

comments.author: Iris Khan
comments.body: Backoff was added but report failed again after a burst of 18 requests.

comments.author: Ravi Kumar
comments.body: Reopening because the same symptom occurred for the third time.

custom_fields.customer_impact: Medium - leadership report was missing

summary: Remove unused notebooks and temporary evaluation artifacts
description: Repository contains old notebooks, scratch JSON dumps, and one local vector store snapshot. Remove files that are not required for app runtime or tests.
acceptance_criteria: Delete only files confirmed unused.
Document removed paths in the cleanup summary.
Tests pass after cleanup.

comments.author: Maya Chen
comments.body: Please avoid deleting sample fixtures used by retrieval tests.

custom_fields.customer_impact: Low - developer experience only

summary: Suggested solution response invents implementation estimates
description: When asked for a solution for AICB-101, the assistant proposed '2 days of backend work' even though the ticket has no estimate field or implementation plan.
acceptance_criteria: Suggested solutions are labeled as recommendations.
Missing estimate or owner information is reported as missing information.
The answer does not invent dates, effort, or assignee details.

comments.author: Ava Patel
comments.body: This is a compliance blocker because estimates looked authoritative.

comments.author: Owen Wright
comments.body: Adding a stricter solution prompt and confidence section.

custom_fields.customer_impact: High - users may act on invented effort estimates
custom_fields.root_cause_hypothesis: Prompt did not forbid unsupported implementation details.

summary: Create risk dashboard for unresolved Jira tickets
description: Product owners need a dashboard that highlights blocked tickets, high priority unresolved tickets, long inactivity, and tickets missing owner or acceptance criteria.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard lists blocked tickets with blocker reason when present.
Dashboard lists high priority unresolved tickets.
Dashboard identifies tickets inactive for more than 14 days.
Dashboard identifies missing assignee and missing acceptance criteria.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.body: Use this dummy dataset as an initial analytics fixture.

custom_fields.customer_impact: Low - internal planning

summary: Jira sync drops comments after the first 100 comments
description: Large tickets with more than 100 comments only ingest the first page of comments. Analysis for long-running incidents misses the latest customer updates.
acceptance_criteria: Comment pagination fetches all comments for each ticket.
Latest comment timestamp is stored in processed ticket metadata.
Sync logs warn when Jira returns partial comment data.

comments.author: Elena Brooks
comments.body: A customer escalation note was missing from the generated answer.

comments.author: Ravi Kumar
comments.body: No assignee yet. This should be triaged today.

custom_fields.customer_impact: High - latest incident details can be omitted

summary: Define ServiceNow to Jira field mapping for imported tickets
description: Imported ServiceNow incidents do not have a finalized mapping for urgency, impact, assignment group, and caller. Requirements are currently unclear.

comments.author: Lina Gomez
comments.body: Blocked until product confirms whether impact maps to priority or severity.

custom_fields.customer_impact: Medium - imported ticket analysis may rank incidents incorrectly
custom_fields.blocked_reason: Product decision needed on field mapping

summary: HTML tags from Jira descriptions appear in chatbot answers
description: Descriptions containing Jira rich text are converted to strings with raw HTML tags. The chatbot sometimes repeats the tags in final answers.
acceptance_criteria: HTML tags are stripped or converted to plain text during preprocessing.
Bullet lists remain readable in the final answer.
Regression test covers Jira rich text fields.

comments.author: Iris Khan
comments.body: Resolved by normalizing Jira rich text before chunking.

custom_fields.customer_impact: Low - answer readability issue

summary: Add similar ticket search for duplicate incident analysis
description: Support engineers need to find tickets similar to a current incident by summary, description, labels, components, and recent comments.
acceptance_criteria: Given a ticket key, return the top five similar unresolved tickets.
Results include similarity reason and cited matching fields.
Duplicate chunks from the same ticket are grouped together.

comments.author: Sam Rivera
comments.body: Reranker improves similar results, but duplicate chunks still need grouping.

custom_fields.customer_impact: Medium - support spends time searching manually

summary: Ticket summary cache is never refreshed after manual resync
description: Manual resync refreshes raw Jira data but does not clear cached generated summaries. Users can see old status and old blocker text in summarized responses.

comments.author: Jordan Lee
comments.body: No update since March. Need to confirm whether this still reproduces.

custom_fields.customer_impact: Low - stale summaries in staging

summary: Imported tickets missing acceptance criteria should be flagged
description: Several imported stories have empty acceptance criteria. The analysis layer should flag these as unclear requirements instead of treating them as ready for implementation.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.body: This should appear in the risk dashboard as missing acceptance criteria and missing owner.

custom_fields.customer_impact: Medium - unclear requirements can enter sprint planning

summary: Prompt sends full ticket JSON and exceeds token budget
description: For broad questions, the answer pipeline sends raw ticket JSON for every retrieved chunk. Long comments and unused fields push requests above the configured token limit.
acceptance_criteria: Prompt context uses compact field blocks instead of raw JSON.
Long comments are summarized before prompt construction.
Context builder enforces a maximum token budget.

comments.author: Ravi Kumar
comments.body: Compact context reduced one sample prompt from 11k tokens to 3.4k tokens.

comments.author: Maya Chen
comments.body: Need final review for comment summarization behavior.

custom_fields.customer_impact: High - users see slow or failed answers

summary: Cache repeated answers for identical safe Jira questions
description: Repeated questions such as 'which high priority tickets are blocked' should reuse a cached answer when the underlying ticket dataset has not changed.
acceptance_criteria: Answer cache key includes normalized question and dataset version.
Cache is bypassed for questions requiring current time-sensitive data.
Logs include answer cache hit or miss without ticket content.

comments.author: Ravi Kumar
comments.body: Use this only when retrieval results are stable and ticket updated_at values are unchanged.

custom_fields.customer_impact: Low - performance improvement

summary: VIP customer cannot query blocker status from chatbot
description: A VIP customer asked which tickets block the June launch, but the chatbot returned a generic answer without listing the blocked tickets. Account team escalated the issue.
acceptance_criteria: Query for blockers returns blocked ticket keys and blocker reasons when available.
Escalated answers include missing information instead of guessing launch impact.
Customer-facing response cites all referenced ticket IDs.

comments.author: Ava Patel
comments.body: Customer asked for launch blockers and got an answer with no ticket IDs.

comments.author: Noah Singh
comments.body: Likely retrieval issue: blocked label was not included in chunk metadata.

comments.author: Ravi Kumar
comments.body: AICB-104 and AICB-113 should have been retrieved for that question.

comments.author: Elena Brooks
comments.body: Please send a corrected summary before the June launch readiness call.

comments.author: Noah Singh
comments.body: Escalation is active until blocker query is fixed and verified.

custom_fields.customer_impact: High - VIP customer escalation
custom_fields.blocked_reason: Blocked ticket retrieval misses status and label metadata
